medical_history.chief_complaint: hepatomegaly, abnormal coagulation, jaundice, and lethargy
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient presented with hepatomegaly, abnormal coagulation, jaundice, and lethargy. Her condition progressively worsened and despite intensive management she died on day 31 of life of sepsis with diffuse, uncontrolled, cutaneous and mucosal bleeding, and multi-organ failure.

physical_examination.general_examination: Post mortem evaluation revealed significant iron deposits in the pancreas, thyroid, heart, and in the liver, which presented extensive loss of parenchyma, and giant cell transformation, while residual hepatocytes showed iron overload. Reticuloendothelium was spared.
